title: Ed Arrival Triage Priority Routing
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: ed arrival triage priority routing.
assumptions: Compatibility relations between patient encounters and resources are precomputed and encoded as predicates.
Staff and equipment competence are adequate for assigned tasks; no competency mismatch will be created at allocation time.
Actions are deterministic: allocations, releases and state transitions succeed when preconditions are met.
Single ED site context; inter-facility transfers are out of scope.
Resource pools are finite and modeled explicitly; concurrent conflicts are resolved by plan sequencing.
risks: Triage misclassification leading to under-triage or over-triage and potential patient harm.
Resource starvation or bottlenecks (e.g., no available beds or clinicians) delaying definitive care.
Incorrect compatibility mapping causing assignment of inappropriate resources.
Race conditions in concurrent assignment/release actions if implemented without locking semantics.
Operational variation (unmodeled interruptions, unexpected arrivals) invalidating planned allocations.
